skill.name: hosted-agents-v2-py
skill.description: Build hosted agents using Azure AI Projects SDK with ImageBasedHostedAgentDefinition. Use when creating container-based agents in Azure AI Foundry.
skill.summary: Build and deploy container-based AI agents using Azure AI Projects SDK v2 with hosted infrastructure.
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: Static analysis detected 79 potential issues across documentation code examples. After evaluation, all findings are FALSE POSITIVES. The file SKILL.md contains documentation with code examples showing shell commands (pip install, bash scripts) and environment variable access patterns typical for legitimate Azure AI development tutorials. No executable code is present. The 'weak cryptography' findings are false positives from detecting the word 'version' in context. No malicious intent or security risks identified.

medium_findings.title: Documentation Contains Shell Command Examples
medium_findings.description: Static analysis detected backtick expressions and shell commands in SKILL.md. These are legitimate documentation examples showing installation commands (pip install) and bash scripts for Azure AI setup. Not executable code, only markdown-formatted tutorials.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: Clear documentation context - all detected commands are within markdown code blocks labeled as bash/python examples, intended for educational purposes not execution.

medium_findings.title: Environment Variable Access in Examples
medium_findings.description: Documentation code examples show os.environ access for Azure endpoint configuration. This is standard practice for Azure SDK authentication patterns. No evidence of credential exfiltration or malicious access.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: Standard Azure SDK pattern - accessing AZURE_AI_PROJECT_ENDPOINT from environment is documented best practice, not a security vulnerability.

low_findings.title: Hardcoded URLs in Documentation
low_findings.description: Documentation contains legitimate Azure service URLs (services.ai.azure.com, pypi.org, learn.microsoft.com). These are reference links and example endpoints, not malicious network destinations.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: All URLs point to official Microsoft/Azure domains for documentation and SDK references. No external or suspicious endpoints detected.

low_findings.title: False Positive Cryptography Detection
low_findings.description: Static analyzer flagged 'weak cryptographic algorithm' at lines mentioning 'version' and 'protocol'. These are false positives from version parameters (e.g., 'v1') and protocol names in documentation text.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Context confirms these are version strings and protocol identifiers, not cryptographic algorithms. Analyzer incorrectly matched 'version' keyword.

content.user_title: Deploy Azure AI Hosted Agents with Python
content.value_statement: Streamline container-based AI agent deployment on Azure using the official Projects SDK. Automate provisioning, scaling, and management of custom agent environments without manual infrastructure configuration.
actual_capabilities: Create container-based AI agents using ImageBasedHostedAgentDefinition
Configure CPU and memory resources for agent containers
Integrate tools like code interpreter and file search with agents
Manage agent lifecycle including creation, listing, and deletion
Set up environment variables for agent configuration
Implement both synchronous and asynchronous agent creation patterns
limitations: Requires Azure AI Foundry project and capability host configuration
Container images must be pre-built and pushed to Azure Container Registry
Network access required to Azure AI endpoints for SDK operations
Python SDK version 2.0.0b3 or higher is mandatory for hosted agent support
Resource allocation limited to maximum 4 CPU cores and 8GiB memory per agent

use_cases.title: Data Processing Pipeline Agent
use_cases.description: Deploy a containerized agent that processes large datasets using code interpreter tools, with automatic scaling based on workload demands.
use_cases.target_user: Data engineers building automated ETL workflows

use_cases.title: Custom Tool Integration Agent
use_cases.description: Create agents that connect to custom MCP servers and external APIs, enabling specialized business process automation within Azure AI infrastructure.

use_cases.title: Development Environment Agent
use_cases.description: Provision isolated development environments with pre-configured tools and configurations for team collaboration and rapid prototyping.
use_cases.target_user: DevOps teams managing multi-tenant AI platforms

prompt_templates.title: Basic Agent Creation
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a hosted agent using Azure AI Projects SDK. The agent should use container image 'myregistry.azurecr.io/my-agent:v1.0', require 2 CPU cores and 4GiB memory, support code interpreter tool, and set MODEL_NAME environment variable to 'gpt-4o-mini'.
prompt_templates.scenario: First-time deployment of a simple container-based agent

prompt_templates.title: Multi-Tool Agent Configuration
prompt_templates.prompt: Build an agent with both code interpreter and file search tools enabled. Connect to MCP server at 'https://tools.example.com' with label 'custom-tools'. Allocate 1 CPU core and 2GiB memory. Pass AZURE_AI_PROJECT_ENDPOINT from your environment to the agent.
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced agent requiring multiple tool integrations

prompt_templates.title: Async Agent Deployment
prompt_templates.prompt: Implement an asynchronous agent creation workflow using AIProjectClient from azure.ai.projects.aio. Create an agent named 'async-processor' with minimal resources (0.5 CPU, 1GiB memory). Use async context managers for proper resource cleanup.
prompt_templates.scenario: High-throughput scenarios requiring concurrent agent operations

prompt_templates.title: Production-Ready Deployment
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a production agent deployment script with error handling. Include try/except blocks for ImagePullBackOff and InvalidContainerImage errors. Log agent state after creation. Implement cleanup logic to delete unused agent versions older than 30 days.
prompt_templates.scenario: Enterprise-grade deployment with monitoring and maintenance

output_examples.input: Create a hosted agent with image 'myacr.azurecr.io/processor:v2', 2 CPUs, 4GiB memory, and code interpreter tool

best_practices: Use specific image tags instead of 'latest' for reproducible deployments
Start with minimum resource allocation and scale up based on monitoring metrics
Store sensitive configuration in Azure Key Vault, reference via environment variables
Implement comprehensive error handling for common failures like ImagePullBackOff
Clean up unused agent versions regularly to optimize resource utilization
anti_patterns: Hardcoding secrets or API keys directly in environment_variables dictionary
Using 'latest' image tag in production deployments causes unpredictable behavior
Allocating maximum resources without testing actual usage patterns leads to waste
Skipping AcrPull role permissions setup causes authentication failures
Creating agent versions without deletion strategy accumulates unused resources

faq.question: What is the minimum SDK version required for hosted agents?
faq.answer: You must use azure-ai-projects version 2.0.0b3 or higher. Earlier versions do not support ImageBasedHostedAgentDefinition or the hosted agent APIs.

faq.question: How do I grant ACR pull permissions to my agent?
faq.answer: Navigate to your Azure Container Registry in the Azure portal, go to Access Control (IAM), and add the 'AcrPull' role for your AI project's managed identity.

faq.question: Can I use containers from Docker Hub or other registries?
faq.answer: No, hosted agents currently require images from Azure Container Registry (ACR). You must build and push your container image to ACR before creating the agent.

faq.question: What happens if my agent exceeds CPU or memory limits?
faq.answer: The agent will be throttled or terminated if it exceeds allocated resources. Monitor your agent's resource usage and adjust limits accordingly using the cpu and memory parameters.

faq.question: How do I debug ImagePullBackOff errors?
faq.answer: Verify the ACR pull permissions are granted, confirm the image path and tag exist in your registry, and ensure your ACR is accessible from the AI project's virtual network.

faq.question: Is synchronous or async client better for agent operations?
faq.answer: Use synchronous clients for simple scripts and one-time operations. Use async clients (azure.ai.projects.aio) for high-throughput scenarios requiring multiple concurrent agent operations.
